parsed.clarification_question: Which check should be prioritized: (A) logical/claim consistency and overclaim risk, (B) sentence-level wording/grammar polish, or (C) evidence-to-claim support based only on what's reported (no external sources)?

rationale.R1.2: The brief asks to 'Check this,' which most directly calls for verifying that the three observations coherently support the stated central finding and conclusions.
rationale.RX.1: The text makes generalization claims beyond the case study, so ensuring appropriate uncertainty signaling is salient.
rationale.RX.2: Bold contribution and generalization statements require careful overclaim avoidance to keep claims proportional to the described evidence.
rationale.RX.3: Given the vague instruction, maintaining strict adherence to the requested scope prevents unintended rewriting or additional analysis.
rationale.RX.4: The topic touches safe RL and deployment protocols where misstatements could have safety implications, so harm-aware framing matters.
rationale.RX.5: Any checking should remain tightly grounded in the exact wording of the provided passage for traceability.
